commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e9d0539ea9461b916e1f4f30a433285505217ea8)
commands.review_summary:
The PR wires iOS chat transport to `sessions.messages.subscribe`, maps `session.message` frames, removes shared view-model filters that dropped assistant transcript events, and adds Swift regression coverage.

PR surface: Other +126. Total +126 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main has an iOS no-op active-session hook, ignores `session.message` frames, and then the shared view model filters out assistant or pending session-message updates. I did not run the app because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Contributor or maintainer action is needed to fix the iOS test target and provide real iOS behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not queue a repair because automation cannot supply the contributor's device/simulator proof.

target.title: fix(ios): subscribe to per-session transcripts so group chats update in real time (#80231)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #80231 â€” Aight (iOS) group chats never surface assistant replies in real time. The user must exit the chat and re-enter for the next agent message to appear. 1-on-1 chats stream normally.

Three compounding causes, all in the iOS/shared event path:

1. **iOS active-session hook is a no-op.** `IOSGatewayChatTransport.setActiveSessionKey` is a comment-only stub. The macOS native transport already calls `sessions.messages.subscribe` with the active session key (`apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/WebChatSwiftUI.swift:140`), but iOS never opts into the per-session transcript stream, so the gateway has no reason to push `session.message` frames to this conn.
2. **iOS drops `session.message` frames.** The iOS event switch maps `tick`/`seqGap`/`health`/`chat`/`agent` and falls through on everything else, so even if the gateway did push transcripts, the iOS transport never decodes them into the shared `OpenClawChatTransportEvent.sessionMessage` case the view model already understands.
3. **Shared `ChatViewModel.handleSessionMessageEvent` filters out assistant transcripts.** `handleSessionMessageEvent` returns early unless the decoded role is `user`, and also short-circuits while `pendingRunCount > 0`. In a group chat the user's send creates one pending run, but N agent replies arrive in parallel as `session.message` events â€” both guards drop them. The 1-on-1 happy path masked this because the local `chat` final event triggers a full history refresh after the single agent reply, but in group chats the assistant transcripts never made it into `vm.messages` until the next bootstrap (exit/re-enter the chat).

This change wires iOS through the existing gateway contract â€” `sessions.messages.subscribe` + `session.message` â€” exactly the way macOS already does, and stops the shared model from discarding assistant transcript updates for the active session. The gateway-side path is unchanged; `src/gateway/server-session-events.ts:142` already emits `session.message` for every role and rolls back to `sessions.changed` when there is nothing to display.

## What changed

- `apps/ios/Sources/Chat/IOSGatewayChatTransport.swift`
  - `setActiveSessionKey` now sends `sessions.messages.subscribe { key }` instead of being a no-op comment.
  - The `events()` event loop now delegates to a static `mapEventFrame(_:)` that maps `session.message` payloads into `.sessionMessage(OpenClawSessionMessageEventPayload)` alongside the existing `tick`/`seqGap`/`health`/`chat`/`agent` cases. The static entry point keeps the mapping unit-testable without standing up a live gateway.
- `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Sources/OpenClawChatUI/ChatViewModel.swift`
  - `handleSessionMessageEvent` no longer returns early on `role != "user"` or on `pendingRunCount > 0`. The remaining `matchesCurrentSessionKey` guard, `stripInboundMetadata`, `reconcileMessageIDs`, and `dedupeMessages` already collapse overlap with the chat-final history refresh, so removing the two guards is what unblocks assistant transcripts without duplicating them.
- `apps/ios/Tests/Logic/IOSGatewayChatTransportTests.swift` (moved out of `apps/ios/Tests/` so it lives in the standalone `OpenClawLogicTests` target alongside the rest of the OpenClawKit-only tests)
  - Existing `requestsFailFastWhenGatewayNotConnected` case now also asserts `setActiveSessionKey` throws when the gateway is disconnected (regression guard for the new RPC).
  - New `mapsSessionMessageEventToSessionMessage`, `mapsChatEventToChat`, `mapsUnknownEventToNil` cases exercise `IOSGatewayChatTransport.mapEventFrame(_:)` directly. The session.message case decodes an `assistant` role payload â€” the exact failure mode the bug report describes.
- `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Tests/OpenClawKitTests/ChatViewModelTests.swift`
  - New `appendsExternalSessionAssistantMessageWhileRunPending` test reproduces the group-chat shape end-to-end on the real shared view model: bootstrap empty history, send a user message (creates a pending run), emit a `session.message` with `role: "assistant"`, assert the assistant message lands in `vm.messages` without leaving the chat.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** in Aight, agent replies inside a group chat do not appear until the user exits and re-enters the conversation. 1-on-1 chats stream normally. (#80231)

**Real environment tested:** macOS arm64 (Darwin 25.3.0, macOS 26.3 25D125), Apple Swift version 6.3.2 (swift-driver 1.148.6), Xcode 16-class toolchain. Real `swift test` against the production `OpenClawChatUI` module (`apps/shared/OpenClawKit`, target macos14) and the production `MacGatewayChatTransport` (`apps/macos`). No mocks for the view model, decoders, transport-event mapping, or AnyCodable plumbing â€” the shared `TestChatTransport` only stands in for the network so the view model can drive its real `OpenClawChatTransportEvent` handling. Baseline run was the same `swift test` invocation against unpatched main (commit `5a33378f9c5`) with the two source files stashed.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```
# Patched branch
git checkout fix/ios-group-chat-realtime-80231

# Shared ChatViewModel regression: focused 3-case run (real model + transport-event handling)
( cd apps/shared/OpenClawKit && \
  swift test --filter 'ChatViewModelTests/(appendsExternalSessionAssistantMessageWhileRunPending|appendsExternalSessionUserMessageForActiveSession|ignoresExternalSessionUserMessageForOtherSession)' )

# Shared ChatViewModel regression: full suite (45 tests) to catch dedupe/refresh side-effects
( cd apps/shared/OpenClawKit && swift test --filter ChatViewModelTests )

# macOS native transport mapping (sibling-surface proof for session.message decode)
( cd apps/macos && swift test --filter MacGatewayChatTransportMappingTests )
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Patched-branch focused run:

```
Build complete! (4.19s)
â—‡ Test run started.
â†³ Testing Library Version: 1902
â†³ Target Platform: arm64e-apple-macos14.0
â—‡ Suite ChatViewModelTests started.
â—‡ Test ignoresExternalSessionUserMessageForOtherSession() started.
â—‡ Test appendsExternalSessionUserMessageForActiveSession() started.
â—‡ Test appendsExternalSessionAssistantMessageWhileRunPending() started.
âœ” Test appendsExternalSessionAssistantMessageWhileRunPending() passed after 0.014 seconds.
âœ” Test appendsExternalSessionUserMessageForActiveSession() passed after 0.014 seconds.
âœ” Test ignoresExternalSessionUserMessageForOtherSession() passed after 0.051 seconds.
âœ” Suite ChatViewModelTests passed after 0.051 seconds.
âœ” Test run with 3 tests in 1 suite passed after 0.051 seconds.
```

Patched-branch full ChatViewModelTests suite:

```
âœ” Suite ChatViewModelTests passed after 0.219 seconds.
âœ” Test run with 45 tests in 1 suite passed after 0.219 seconds.
```

Patched-branch macOS transport mapping:

```
âœ” Test "session message event maps to session message" passed after 0.003 seconds.
âœ” Suite MacGatewayChatTransportMappingTests passed after 0.003 seconds.
âœ” Test run with 7 tests in 1 suite passed after 0.003 seconds.
```

Same focused command on unpatched `5a33378f9c5` (only the two source-file diffs stashed; the new view-model test is present so the failure mode is the bug):

```
â—‡ Test appendsExternalSessionUserMessageForActiveSession() started.
â—‡ Test appendsExternalSessionAssistantMessageWhileRunPending() started.
â—‡ Test ignoresExternalSessionUserMessageForOtherSession() started.
âœ” Test appendsExternalSessionUserMessageForActiveSession() passed after 0.013 seconds.
âœ” Test ignoresExternalSessionUserMessageForOtherSession() passed after 0.054 seconds.
âœ˜ Test appendsExternalSessionAssistantMessageWhileRunPending() recorded an issue at ChatViewModelTests.swift:855:6: Caught error: Timeout waiting for: assistant transcript visible while pending
âœ˜ Test appendsExternalSessionAssistantMessageWhileRunPending() failed after 3.014 seconds with 1 issue.
âœ˜ Suite ChatViewModelTests failed after 3.014 seconds with 1 issue.
âœ˜ Test run with 3 tests in 1 suite failed after 3.014 seconds with 1 issue.
```

The 3.014s timeout on unpatched main is the issue's exact symptom replayed in a unit harness: the assistant `session.message` is delivered to the shared view model and never lands in `vm.messages`, so the test waits the full `waitUntil` budget. After the patch the same emit lands the assistant transcript in `vm.messages` within 14 ms.

**Observed result after fix:**

- `appendsExternalSessionAssistantMessageWhileRunPending`: assistant `session.message` event delivered during a pending run is now visible in `vm.messages` (0.014 s on patched main; 3.014 s timeout on unpatched main).
- `appendsExternalSessionUserMessageForActiveSession` and `ignoresExternalSessionUserMessageForOtherSession`: existing user-role and cross-session guards still hold.
- Full `ChatViewModelTests` suite passes (45/45, 0.219 s) â€” dedupe, message-id reconciliation, and chat-final refresh paths are not regressed.
- `MacGatewayChatTransportMappingTests` passes (7/7, 0.003 s) â€” the macOS sibling surface that already implements the same contract continues to map `session.message` correctly.

**What was not tested:**

- iOS app build / Aight on-device or on-simulator run. `pnpm ios:build` requires the watchOS 26.5 SDK (the OpenClaw scheme embeds the WatchKit app) and the iOS Apple Development signing identity matching `Y5PE65HELJ`; neither is installed in this environment. The standalone `OpenClawLogicTests` target also failed to compile via `xcodebuild -sdk iphonesimulator` with `unable to resolve module dependency: 'ElevenLabsKit'` â€” a pre-existing explicit-modules iOS build issue that reproduces on unpatched main and is not caused by this change. The iOS transport mapping is unit-tested via `IOSGatewayChatTransport.mapEventFrame(_:)` in the new `apps/ios/Tests/Logic/IOSGatewayChatTransportTests.swift`, structurally mirroring the (passing) macOS mapping test.
- Live multi-agent Aight group-chat repro on a real device. Recommend a maintainer with a working iOS build chain run `pnpm ios:build` + a 2-agent group chat after this lands to capture the end-to-end visual proof.
target.author: yetval
